title: Lima 7-Day Food & Culture Tour
metaTitle: Lima 7-Day Food & Culture Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Lima, Peru: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Museo Larco and Huaca Pucllana. Prices from $1052 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Lima, Peru — Mild, low-latitude desert climate moderated by the cold Humboldt Current — warm and sunnier December-April (highs 26-28°C) and notably cool, gray and overcast May-November (highs 18-19°C) despite being near the equator; rainfall is minimal year-round. December to April brings the warmest, sunniest weather Lima gets, since the city is famously overcast much of the rest of the year due to coastal fog (garúa). This itinerary blends Museo Larco, Huaca Pucllana, Malecón de Miraflores with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Museo Larco, Huaca Pucllana, Malecón de Miraflores
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Lima
itinerary.description: Land at Jorge Chávez International Airport (LIM) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Museo Larco — Pre-Columbian art museum in an 18th-century viceroy's mansion with erotic-art gallery. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Lima: Huaca Pucllana & Circuito Mágico del Agua
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Huaca Pucllana — Pre-Incan adobe pyramid ruins in the middle of Miraflores. In the afternoon, continue to Circuito Mágico del Agua — Massive fountain park with nighttime light and water shows. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Lima: Museo de Arte de Lima (MALI) & Catacombs of the Monasterio de San Francisco
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Museo de Arte de Lima (MALI) — Peru's premier art museum spanning pre-Columbian to contemporary. In the afternoon, continue to Catacombs of the Monasterio de San Francisco — Colonial monastery with catacomb crypts beneath. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Catacombs of the Monasterio de San Francisco

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Pachacamac archaeological site
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Pachacamac archaeological site, a popular side trip from Lima. Return to Lima in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Pachacamac archaeological site

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Parque El Olivar centuries-old olive grove — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Parque El Olivar centuries-old olive grove

itinerary.title: Lima: Paragliding over Miraflores/Costa Verde & Ceviche and Pisco cooking class
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Paragliding over Miraflores/Costa Verde — Tandem paraglide along the coastal cliffs. In the afternoon, continue to Ceviche and Pisco cooking class — Hands-on class covering Peru's national dish and spirit. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Museo Nacional de Arqueología, Antropología e Historia or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to LIM for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book Central or Maido weeks to months in advance — both are consistently ranked among the world's best restaurants.
Base yourself in Miraflores or Barranco — both are walkable, safe and close to the best food and views.
Don't judge the weather by the season alone — Lima's coastal fog (garúa) keeps it gray May-November even without rain.
Try ceviche at lunch, when it's traditionally prepared freshest, rather than at dinner.
Use a rideshare app rather than hailing street taxis for a more predictable, safer fare and route.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Layers, even in summer — coastal evenings and the persistent garúa fog can feel cooler than expected
A light jacket for the overcast, cool months (May-November)
Comfortable shoes for extensive walking in Miraflores and Barranco
Sunscreen for clear summer days, despite the city's reputation for gray skies

thingsToAvoid: Walking between districts (e.g., central Lima to Miraflores) on foot or via unfamiliar routes — distances and safety vary by neighborhood.
Carrying obvious valuables or a lot of cash in central Lima or crowded markets.
Assuming sunny weather in the middle of the year — pack layers regardless of season given the garúa fog.
Drinking tap water — stick to bottled or filtered water throughout the city.
Skipping a guide or organized tour in the historic center after dark.
topRestaurants: Isolina Taberna Peruana — Traditional Criollo Peruvian ($$)

Ceviche at a local cevichería for a few dollars
Free wander through Barranco's street art and Bridge of Sighs
Walk the free Miraflores Malecón coastal parks

festivalCalendar.name: Señor de los Milagros procession
festivalCalendar.timing: October, one of the largest religious processions in the Americas

localEtiquette: Greeting with a handshake (or a light cheek kiss between women/mixed pairs who know each other) is standard.
Tipping 10% at restaurants is appreciated and not always included — check for "servicio" on the bill.
Lima's food scene is a point of immense local pride — showing genuine interest in ceviche or Peruvian cuisine is an easy conversation starter.
Punctuality is more relaxed than in some cultures — arriving a little late to social gatherings is normal.
Bargaining is expected at craft markets but not in restaurants or fixed-price shops.
localTransportGuide: The Metropolitano bus rapid transit line is fast and cheap along its main corridor, but rideshare apps are the most practical way to reach Miraflores, Barranco and San Isidro given Lima's sprawling traffic. Walking is excellent within Miraflores and Barranco themselves, which are the two most visitor-friendly, walkable districts.
bestTimeToVisit: December to April brings the warmest, sunniest weather Lima gets, since the city is famously overcast much of the rest of the year due to coastal fog (garúa).
weather: Mild, low-latitude desert climate moderated by the cold Humboldt Current — warm and sunnier December-April (highs 26-28°C) and notably cool, gray and overcast May-November (highs 18-19°C) despite being near the equator; rainfall is minimal year-round.
visaInformation: Visa-free entry for most Western passport holders (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia) for tourist stays up to 90 days. Indian nationals typically need a Peruvian visa arranged in advance of travel.
averageFlightTime: ~18.1h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Lima?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry for most Western passport holders (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia) for tourist stays up to 90 days. Indian nationals typically need a Peruvian visa arranged in advance of travel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Lima?
faqs.answer: December to April brings the warmest, sunniest weather Lima gets, since the city is famously overcast much of the rest of the year due to coastal fog (garúa). Mild, low-latitude desert climate moderated by the cold Humboldt Current — warm and sunnier December-April (highs 26-28°C) and notably cool, gray and overcast May-November (highs 18-19°C) despite being near the equator; rainfall is minimal year-round.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Lima safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Lima is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Lima?
faqs.answer: The local currency is PEN. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Marco Smith
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Museo Nacional de Arqueología, Antropología e Historia and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Grace Larsen
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Plaza Mayor (Plaza de Armas) de Lima and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Ava Brown
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Ceviche and Pisco cooking class alone was worth the trip, but combined with Museo de Arte de Lima (MALI) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Emma Kowalski
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Our trip to Lima was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Ceviche and Pisco cooking class was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Priya Smith
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Gold Museum of Peru (Museo Oro del Perú) in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.
